title: Coryphopteris hirsutipes
taxonomy: Coryphopteris hirsutipes is classified within the family Dryopteridaceae, which is a group of ferns. This classification is based on the plant's physical characteristics and genetic traits. The genus Coryphopteris is a group of ferns that are primarily native to tropical regions. Coryphopteris hirsutipes is specifically a species within the genus Coryphopteris. Further classification includes the order Polypodiales and the division Pteridophyta.
uses: Coryphopteris hirsutipes has been traditionally used in Southeast Asian folk medicine for various purposes. Its leaves are reportedly used to treat fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions. In traditional medicine, it is also used to alleviate digestive issues and to treat skin problems. However, more research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Coryphopteris hirsutipes for medicinal purposes. It is recommended to consult with a healthcare professional before using any plant-based remedies.

parts: Coryphopteris hirsutipes is a plant species that is used in traditional medicine, particularly in the Pacific Islands. The medicinal parts of the plant, such as the leaves, are used to treat various health conditions. preparations: Coryphopteris hirsutipes is a plant species that has been used in traditional medicine, and herbal preparations are made from its leaves and stems. intro: Coryphopteris hirsutipes, also known as the Hirsute Coryphopteris or Hairy Coryphopteris, is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in Southeast Asian medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory infections, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Coryphopteris hirsutipes include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, this plant is used to make herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being incorporated into traditional remedies such as cough syrups and expectorants.
